content: Hi @Asahi Lina ! I'm trying to start making a couple of tests for vgem on rust and I would like to use your working tree, as I will be using a bunch of your bindings. I was trying to use the "gpu/rust-wip" branch, but I'm getting some errors related to the OF. Could you please tell me what branch you are currently using?
That's the branch! What errors are you getting? It doesn't get much build testing outside of the specific configs we use, so it could well have something broken...

Asahi Lina said:

That's the branch! What errors are you getting? It doesn't get much build testing outside of the specific configs we use, so it could well have something broken...

I 'm getting the following errors using x86_64 defconfig + CONFIG_RUST=y: https://pastebin.com/Z4GaxyCs

Ohh that happens with !CONFIG_OF_DYNAMIC because those functions turn into static inline so they'd need helpers... I think I fixed it, can you update? You might also be interested in gpu/explicit-sync which has dma_fence, syncobj, and gpu_scheduler abstractions now (completely untested though, I only just got all the abstraction + driver changes to compile, I'll test/debug it on Friday!)
The update I pushed today includes the dma_fence_ops implementation, by the way ^^

Asahi Lina said:

Ohh that happens with !CONFIG_OF_DYNAMIC because those functions turn into static inline so they'd need helpers... I think I fixed it, can you update? You might also be interested in gpu/explicit-sync which has dma_fence, syncobj, and gpu_scheduler abstractions now (completely untested though, I only just got all the abstraction + driver changes to compile, I'll test/debug it on Friday!)

Thanks for fixing it! But now I'm getting new problems yet related to OF: https://pastebin.com/RB4U0UTc. I guess some #[cfg(CONFIG_OF)] are still missing.

Asahi Lina said:

The update I pushed today includes the dma_fence_ops implementation, by the way ^^

I watched a bit of the stream today and they are looking neat. I'll definitely check the new branch!

I think before you might have had CONFIG_OF enabled but not CONFIG_OF_DYNAMIC, and now you have CONFIG_OF disabled too perhaps? Yes, without CONFIG_OF everything becomes an inline dummy function that needs a wrapper or a conditional in the code... I hope we get that bindgen feature to autogenerate the wrappers soon ^^

In the meantime you can probably just delete everything in of.rs starting at pub struct Node. I'll have to figure out how to handle this when I rebase/submit it...
